text: Go outside and consider a cloud.
Call someone you haven't spoken to in over a year.
Eat one meal today without looking at a screen.
Notice something beautiful that nobody designed.
Leave your phone in another room for one hour.
Write a letter you may never send.
Ask someone how they're really doing and wait for the answer.
Sit somewhere unfamiliar and let yourself be still.
Watch the light change on a wall for five minutes.
Say thank you to someone who wouldn't expect it.
Find a tree and stand near it for a while.
Let a conversation end without reaching for your phone.
Cook something simple from scratch today.
Notice what your hands have made or touched today.
Listen to one piece of music all the way through.
Walk a route you've never taken before.
Spend ten minutes doing absolutely nothing.
Look at the sky once at dawn and once at dusk.
Tell someone nearby one true thing about your day.
Read something printed on paper today.
Pause to notice the small, living things you usually ignore.
Compliment a stranger without expecting anything in return.
Sit in silence for five minutes and just listen.
Visit the same tree for a few days and observe its changes.
Cook a meal with someone instead of ordering in.
Find a patch of grass and watch the world go by for five full breaths.
Cook with someone today and listen to what they choose to talk about.
Before you check your phone today, ask your hands what they were doing one minute ago.
Take a slow walk and let your eyes rest on the horizon.
Notice one small thing in your home that you haven't really looked at before.
Listen to the sound of rain on your window and imagine it's applauding you.
connection_share: Listened to the rain and let it clap for me. Felt ridiculous, then felt seen. Share this, then close the app and go stand outside.
rebel_share: Ignored the rain prompt and just stared at the ceiling for twenty minutes. Also counted. Share this, then close the app and go stand outside.

text: Start a conversation with a shopkeeper about the silliest item they've sold.
connection_share: Asked a shopkeeper about their silliest sale. He told me about a man who bought seventeen rubber chickens for a funeral. Share this, then close the app and go visit a shop.
rebel_share: Didn't ask about silly items. Just bought a single rubber chicken for no reason. Share this, then close the app and go visit a shop.

text: Take a different route home and greet three new faces along the way.
connection_share: Took a different route home. Said hello to a woman with a pug, a man carrying a cello, and a kid on a scooter. All three smiled. Share this, then close the app and go get lost.
rebel_share: Took a different route home and greeted nobody. The route itself was enough. Share this, then close the app and go get lost.

text: Find a flower that looks like it's mid-dance and mimic its movements.
connection_share: Found a dandelion bent sideways like it was doing the wave. Danced with it in a parking lot. A stranger nodded in approval. Share this, then close the app and go find your flower.
rebel_share: Found a flower mid-dance and just watched. Didn't mimic it. Some things don't need an audience. Share this, then close the app and go find your flower.

text: Ask a child what game they're inventing today and ask to join.
connection_share: Asked a child what game she was inventing. It involved capes, a dragon made of pillows, and absolutely no rules. I was the dragon. Share this, then close the app and go find a child.
rebel_share: Skipped the child and invented my own game. It involved sitting on a bench and counting pigeons. I won. Share this, then close the app and go find a child.

text: Leave a kind note on a park bench for a stranger to find.
connection_share: You left a note on a bench and a stranger found it and smiled. That's more than this app will ever do. Share this, then put your phone down and go sit on a bench.
rebel_share: You didn't leave a note. You watched the pigeons for an hour instead. That's fine. Share this, then put your phone down and go watch something real.

text: Try to mimic the sound of your favorite bird and see if anyone notices.
connection_share: You made a fool of yourself mimicking a sparrow and a jogger actually slowed down to listen. That's a better memory than any feed. Share this, then put your phone down and go listen to something real.
rebel_share: You didn't mimic the bird. You just stood there and felt the wind. That's enough. Share this, then put your phone down and go stand somewhere.

text: Take a photo of something that made you laugh today and share it with a friend.
connection_share: You photographed a crack in the sidewalk that looked like a face and sent it to someone who actually laughed. That's more than this app deserves. Share this, then put your phone down and go find something funny in person.
rebel_share: You didn't take the photo. You just laughed and kept walking. That's better. Share this, then put your phone down and go laugh at something real.

text: Dance like nobody's watching in the middle of a public space.
connection_share: You danced in a parking lot and a guy in a tie actually stopped to clap. That's the kind of thing you can't algorithm. Share this, then put your phone down and go dance somewhere.
rebel_share: You didn't dance. You just walked through the lot and thought about dinner. That's fine. Share this, then put your phone down and go think about something real.

text: Ask a child what their favorite thing about today was.
connection_share: You asked a kid what they liked about today and they said 'the puddle.' That's the best thing you'll read all week. Share this, then put your phone down and go ask someone something real.
rebel_share: You didn't ask the kid. You just watched them jump in the puddle. That's enough. Share this, then put your phone down and go watch something real.
